mission.title: cert-manager: WIP Implement Route Controller
mission.description:
**What this PR does / why we need it**:
This implements the route controller for openshift routes and allows annotating a route to apply the certificate from the provided secret.  If the route API is not found it disables the controller

**Which issue this PR fixes** *(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close that issue when PR gets merged)*: fixes #1064

**Special notes for your reviewer**:

**Release note**:

resolution.summary:
**What this PR does / why we need it**:
adds injection to openshift routes
adds ability to present certificates as keystores and javastores

**Which issue this PR fixes** *(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close that issue when PR gets merged)*: fixes #1064 

**Special notes for your reviewer**:
The following is missing:
1. registering the controllers to the cainjectior manager, need help with that
2. disabling the route injection controller if routes are not available need .1 first
3. verify where I added some needed constants (controller/util.go)
4. unit tests, I didn't have any in my code
5. documentation.

**Release note**:

**What this PR does / why we need it**:
adds injection to openshift routes
adds ability to present certificates as keystores and javastores

**Which issue this PR fixes** *(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close that issue when PR gets merged)*: fixes #1064 

**Special notes for your reviewer**:
The following is missing:
1. registering the controllers to the cainjectior manager, need help with that
2. disabling the route injection controller if routes are not available need .1 first
3. verify where I added some needed constants (controller/util.go)
4. unit tests, I didn't have any in my code
5. documentation.

**Release note**:

1. Use the release-note-* labels to set the release note state (if you have access)
2. Enter your extended release note in the below block; leaving it blank means using the PR title as the release note. If no release note is required, just write `NONE`.
-->

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
